title: carex glauca f. subustulata
intro: Carex glauca f. subustulata, also known as the rusty-fruit sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae), native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Carex glauca f. subustulata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
